reasoning: To resolve 'Yes', June 2025 must record a temperature anomaly strictly greater than the record of 107 (1.07°C) set in June 2024. During 2024, a strong El Niño pushed global temperatures to unprecedented highs. By mid-2025, global temperatures have cooled slightly relative to those peaks due to the fading of El Niño and the emergence of La Niña conditions. Data for early 2025 shows a cooling trend relative to the same months in 2024 (e.g., April 2025 was 0.07°C cooler than April 2024). Furthermore, the 2% Polymarket price as of mid-June 2025 strongly indicates that real-time daily temperature trackers (like ERA5) are showing June 2025 tracking comfortably below the record levels set in June 2024. Therefore, it is highly unlikely that June 2025 will exceed the June 2024 record.
